examples: Would you please turn that stereo off and get your homework done?

phrasalVerbs.definition: develop in a particular way or have a particular result
examples: I was late for the interview, but it turned out all right in the end and I got the job.

phrasalVerbs.definition: turn a page so that the other side is towards you
examples: If you all turn over, you’ll see a table that shows how sales have gone this year.

phrasalVerbs.definition: go back in the opposite direction
examples: I realised I’d forgotten my phone so I turned round and went back to get it.

phrasalVerbs.definition: appear unexpectedly or without making a firm arrangement
examples: Victor hadn’t been invited to the party but he just turned up anyway.

phrasalVerbs.definition: stop being effective (for a drug, etc)
examples: My arm started to hurt again as the effects of the painkiller wore off.
I had to get a new pair of trainers because my old pair had worn out.

phrasalVerbs.definition: find the solution to a problem, etc
examples: If you look at the context, maybe you can work out what the word means.
